---
title: "Lecture Slides"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-section">
  <h1>Lecture Slides for<br/>Machine Learning Systems</h1>
  <p class="hero-subtitle">
    Beamer LaTeX slide decks with speaker notes, active learning, and 266 original SVG diagrams.
    Free to download, customize, and teach from.
  </p>

  <div class="hero-stats">
    <div class="hero-stat">
      <span class="stat-number">35</span>
      <span class="stat-label">Slide Decks</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hero-stat">
      <span class="stat-number">1,099</span>
      <span class="stat-label">Slides</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hero-stat">
      <span class="stat-number">266</span>
      <span class="stat-label">SVG Figures</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hero-stat">
      <span class="stat-number">~38 hrs</span>
      <span class="stat-label">Teaching Time</span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div>
    <a href="#" class="btn-crimson">Download All (Vol I + II)</a>
    <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/cs249r_book/tree/main/slides" class="btn-outline">View Source on GitHub</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="vol-cards">
  <div class="vol-card">
    <h3>Volume I: Foundations</h3>
    <p>
      <strong>Introduction to Machine Learning Systems</strong> &mdash;
      17 decks covering the full single-machine ML stack from data engineering to deployment.
      570 slides, 141 SVGs.
    </p>
    <div class="vol-meta">
      Chapters 0&ndash;16 &middot; ~19 hours of lectures &middot; 145 active learning moments
    </div>
    <div style="margin-top: 1rem;">
      <a href="vol1.html" class="btn-crimson">Browse Vol I Decks</a>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="vol-card">
    <h3>Volume II: At Scale</h3>
    <p>
      <strong>Machine Learning Systems at Scale</strong> &mdash;
      18 decks covering distributed training, fleet orchestration, inference at scale, and governance.
      529 slides, 125 SVGs.
    </p>
    <div class="vol-meta">
      Chapters 0&ndash;17 &middot; ~19 hours of lectures &middot; 163 active learning moments
    </div>
    <div style="margin-top: 1rem;">
      <a href="vol2.html" class="btn-crimson">Browse Vol II Decks</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="info-section">
  <h2>For Instructors</h2>
  <p>
    These slides are <strong>Beamer LaTeX source</strong>, not locked PDFs. Fork the repository,
    edit the <code>.tex</code> files, and rebuild with <code>make</code>. The SVG figures are
    the source of truth for all diagrams; the theme file
    (<code>assets/beamerthememlsys.sty</code>) controls colors, fonts, and layout globally.
  </p>
  <p>
    Every slide has speaker notes with timing, teaching guidance, and common student errors.
    Active learning moments (predict, exercise, discussion, retrieval practice, muddiest point)
    are built into every deck.
  </p>
  <p>
    See the <a href="teaching.html">Teaching Guide</a> for semester plans, customization tips,
    and pedagogical notes.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="info-section">
  <h2>Audio Lectures <span class="coming-soon">Coming Soon</span></h2>
  <p>
    AI-generated narration synchronized to each slide deck, based on the speaker notes
    written by the textbook author. Designed for self-paced learning and flipped classroom use.
  </p>
</div>
```
